title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: Health departments commonly seek clusters cases space time, cases occurring within similar time frames nearby locations signal outbreaks events public health importance. input data intended current package typically counts outcomes syndromic categories emergency department visits selected infectious diseases, overdoses, harms. However, package may applied seek clusters types count data whose observations space time descriptors. following paragraphs briefly summarize cluster-finding method. scan statistic widely used method detecting clusters. given outcome, method works testing many candidate regions high observed counts outcome identifying regions unusually high counts relative expected levels. many applications including current one, expected counts derived baseline set historical data. anomalous regions evaluated statistical significance potential alerting. approach implemented SaTScan software (www.satscan.org), uses moving cylindrical window space time. base cylinder defines geographic area, height represents time interval. cylinder, observed number cases compared expected count using test statistic. cylinder maximum value scan statistic defines candidate cluster. distribution maximum scan statistic available closed form, SaTScan estimates statistical significance using Monte Carlo simulation. , repeatedly generates simulated datasets null model compares observed cluster reference distribution. approach implemented package replaces repeated simulations immediate significance assessment based spline functions trained large set archived SaTScan results. user chooses spline corresponding significance level; spline defines threshold possible observed count. candidate cluster, alert issued observed count exceeds corresponding threshold. method reduces computation time providing practical approximation standard SaTScan significance test, enabling rapid, -demand cluster detection across outcomes time scales. approach faster using conventional repeated trials provides epidemiologists proxy method obtain clusters demand, outcome, duration. Requirements scan statistic implementation : assignment observed data geographic regions centroid point locations distance calculations enable expanding cylinder scans observed counts data region time step corresponding expected counts data, estimated baseline data distances regions

title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: current package provides utilities cluster detection location-date-count surveillance data. supports: building location distance structures (matrix sparse list forms), computing baseline/test summaries visualization-ready outputs, running full clustering workflow find_clusters(), using lower-level functions step-wise control needed, simulating cluster injections testing workflows. vignette demonstrates exported functions, emphasis recommended wrapper: find_clusters().

what: Technical Requirements to Enable Cluster Detection in User Data
title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: find_clusters() expects data frame least columns: location: character location identifier, date: Date (IDate) values, count: non-negative counts. Dates locations zero counts need provided. many outcomes, counts zero majority locations dates locations. package code accounts date/location pairs. code restricts data 12 locations concise demonstration. Using full dataset impact computation speed. Date helper functions:
code: # use the built in example_count_data  cases <- data.table::as.data.table(example_count_data)  # For demonstration purposes, use only 12 unique locations locs <- unique(cases$location)[1:12] cases <- cases[location %in% locs]  # For demonstration purposes only, keep the final 120 days only cases <- cases[date >= (max(date) - 120)]  # Make sure that location is a character column cases[, location := as.character(location)]  head(cases) #>    location       date count #>      <char>     <IDat> <int> #> 1:    39001 2024-10-08     4 #> 2:    39001 2024-10-09     1 #> 3:    39001 2024-10-10     0 #> 4:    39001 2024-10-11     2 #> 5:    39001 2024-10-12     0 #> 6:    39003 2024-10-08    13 detect_date <- max(cases$date) get_test_dates(detect_date, test_length = 7) #> [1] "2025-01-30" "2025-01-31" "2025-02-01" "2025-02-02" "2025-02-03" #> [6] "2025-02-04" "2025-02-05" ## For reference, but not usually needed in application: head(get_baseline_dates(detect_date, test_length = 7, baseline_length = 90, guard = 0)) #> [1] "2024-11-01" "2024-11-02" "2024-11-03" "2024-11-04" "2024-11-05" #> [6] "2024-11-06"

title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: scanning process requires distances locations enable systematic scanning expanding cylinders space. package provides two ways provide distances:

title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: functions tract_distance_matrix(), zip_distance_matrix() county_distance_matrix() provide full pairwise distance matrices, may sparse many zeros, depending user data: us_distance_matrix() special function available generating pairwise distance U.S. counties, typically large routine examples:
code: zip_dm <- zip_distance_matrix("DC") dim(zip_dm$distance_matrix) #> [1] 246 246  county_dm <- county_distance_matrix("RI", source = "tigris") dim(county_dm$distance_matrix) #> [1] 5 5 us_dm <- us_distance_matrix() dim(us_dm$distance_matrix)

title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: applications many hundreds locations, package function create_dist_list() returns sparse neighbor representation. output function list vectors containing nearby neighbor locations data location, “nearby” means within user-provided threshold. example returns list counties within 25 miles every county Rhode Island. list adequate detection clusters whose radius 25 miles. many outcomes, users seek clusters whose geographic extent exceed threshold. summary, matrix functions return pairwise distances large, square matrix, create_dist_list() returns sparse list within-threshold neighbors (sparse list). sparse lists often faster/lighter large geographies.
code: county_list <- create_dist_list(   level = "county",   st = "RI",   threshold = 25 ) length(county_list) #> [1] 5 head(county_list[[1]]) #>    44009    44005    44003  #>  0.00000 18.89133 19.34145

title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: primary user-facing function find_clusters. function executes following component steps: build baseline/test count grids, aggregate nearby-location counts distance, compute observed expected values, apply spline-derived significance thresholds, compress candidate clusters remove less significant ones avoid overlap, append location-level counts retained clusters.

title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: distance object (matrix sparse list), location names must match location column case data.
code: locs <- unique(cases$location) dm <- outer(seq_along(locs), seq_along(locs), function(i, j) abs(i - j) * 5) dimnames(dm) <- list(locs, locs)

what: Built-In Spline Tables and Example Data
title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: package ships spline lookup tables. observed cluster counts, tables provide significance thresholds approximating p-values 0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05: spline_001 spline_005 spline_01 spline_05 demonstration data tables county zip code locations shape files: example_count_data counties zipcodes can used directly workflows, examples, tests.

title: Vignette 1: Package Overview
Vignette 1: Package Overview
text: Create statistical summary input data: Create visual heatmap summary input data assess suitability cluster detection. plot illustrates spread data counts locations: Create time series aggregated data counts locations:
code: summary_tbl <- generate_summary_table(   data = cases,   end_date = detect_date,   baseline_length = 90,   test_length = 7 ) summary_tbl #>          Statistic (rounded means) Baseline Interval Test Interval #>                             <char>             <num>         <num> #>  1:                      Nr. Dates              90.0             7 #>  2:                Nr. Total Cases            6683.0          1449 #>  3:                  Cases per Day              74.3           207 #>  4:        Nr. Locations with Data              12.0            12 #>  5:      Nr. Locations, no records               0.0             0 #>  6: Nr. Locs, daily mean 1 or less               1.0             1 #>  7:         Nr. Locs, daily mean 2               2.0             1 #>  8:       Nr. Locs, daily mean 3-5               4.0             0 #>  9:      Nr. Locs, daily mean 6-10               2.0             5 #> 10:       Nr. Locs, daily mean >10               3.0             5 heatmap_data <- generate_heatmap_data(   data = cases,   end_date = detect_date,   baseline_length = 90,   test_length = 7 )  p_heat <- generate_heatmap(heatmap_data, plot_type = "plotly") class(p_heat) #> [1] "plotly"     "htmlwidget" p_heat ts_data <- generate_time_series_data(   data = cases,   end_date = detect_date,   baseline_length = 90,   test_length = 7 )  p_ts <- generate_time_series_plot(ts_data, plot_type = "plotly") class(p_ts) #> [1] "plotly"     "htmlwidget" p_ts

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {gsClusterDetect: Utilities for Geo-Spatial Cluster Detection and Significance Classification},   author = {Luke Mullany and Howard Burkom},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://github.com/lmullany/gsClusterDetect}, }

title: Utilities for Geo-Spatial Cluster Detection and Significance Classification
text: R package implementing geospatial cluster identification time series counts, location. Locations can expressed counties, zip codes, census tracts, user-defined geographies. Users provide: data.frame counts location date distance object, contains distance location neighbors package provides functions create distance objects either matrix list format. can generated census tract, zip codes, counties (fips), can constructed custom locations providing dataframe columns latitude longitude (.e centroid location).

title: Utilities for Geo-Spatial Cluster Detection and Significance Classification
text: Install gsClusterDetect package CRAN follows: Install development version Github follows:

title: Utilities for Geo-Spatial Cluster Detection and Significance Classification
text: Load package provide data frame location, date, count columns. Generate distance matrix location. case, synthetic data counts counties/fips state OHIO, use county_distance_matrix() pass state abbreviation: Set end target period. called detect_date, parameter must passed find_clusters function. Typically, might current (last available) date. Call find_clusters() function; See ?find_clusters() full set options. Note , pass minimum required elements: cases, distance_matrix, detect_date, set distance_limit (maximum size clusters) 50 (miles).
code: library(gsClusterDetect) df <- example_count_data tail(df)     location       date count      <char>     <IDat> <int> 1:    39171 2025-02-04     1 2:    39171 2025-02-05     0 3:    39173 2025-02-04     6 4:    39173 2025-02-05     7 5:    39175 2025-02-04     2 6:    39175 2025-02-05     0 ohio_dm <- county_distance_matrix("OH")  # This is named list of two elements cat("Class:", class(ohio_dm), "\nNames:", names(ohio_dm))  Class: list  Names: loc_vec distance_matrix detect_date <- max(df[, date]) clusters <- find_clusters(     cases = df,     distance_matrix = ohio_dm[["distance_matrix"]],     detect_date = detect_date,     distance_limit = 50 )

title: Utilities for Geo-Spatial Cluster Detection and Significance Classification

title: Compress a cluster_alert_table — compress_clusters
text: Function reduces object class `ClusterAlertTable“ final set clusters locations. idea function retain significant, non-overlapping clusters cluster alert table. surrogate significance 'alertGap', log(observed/expected) minus threshold spline assigns observed value. logic function keeps two running tables, table `dt_keep` clusters kept, descending order `alertGap`, `dt_clust`, remaining rows cluster alert table, reduced time cluster accepted `dt_keep`. row cluster alert table represents candidate cluster, column `target`, cluster center, column `location`, distant location center. time cluster accepted `dt_keep`, remaining rows `dt_clust` dropped either `target` `location` center newly accepted cluster. `dt_keep`

what: Get distance matrix for counties within a state — county_distance_matrix
title: Get distance matrix for counties within a state — county_distance_matrix
text: Function returns list counties matrix distance counties. leverages built dataset (`counties`).

title: Get distance matrix for counties within a state — county_distance_matrix
code: county_distance_matrix(   st,   unit = c("miles", "kilometers", "meters"),   source = c("tigris", "rnssp") )

title: Get distance matrix for counties within a state — county_distance_matrix
text: st two-character string denoting state, "US". "US", equivalent calling us_distance_matrix(). unit string, one "miles" (default), "kilometers", "meters". Indicating desired unit distances source string indicating either "tigris" (default) "rnssp". built-datasets (.e. part package). default ("tigris") uses county names locations found tigris 2024. "rnssp" option uses package-stored version publicly available shape file counties Rnssp package https://cdcgov.github.io/Rnssp/

title: Get distance matrix for counties within a state — county_distance_matrix
text: named list length two; first element (`loc_vec`) vector   locations second element (`distance_matrix`) square matrix   containing pairwise distance (given `unit`)   locations.

Reasonable thresholds might 50 (miles), 15 (miles) 3 (miles) county, zip, tract, respectively, can adjusted. Note different unit miles used, user also adjust parameter appropriately st string; optional specify state; NULL distances returned zip codes counties US county string vector 3-fips restrict within st; ignored unless level "tract" unit string one miles (default), kilometers, meters; unit relevant threshold

title: Find clusters — find_clusters
text: Function return clusters, given frame case counts location date, distance matrix, spline lookup table, parameters

title: Find clusters — find_clusters
code: find_clusters(   cases,   distance_matrix,   detect_date,   spline_lookup = NULL,   baseline_length = 90,   max_test_window_days = 7,   guard_band = 0,   distance_limit = 15,   baseline_adjustment = c("add_one", "add_one_global", "add_test", "none"),   adj_constant = 1,   min_clust_cases = 0,   max_clust_cases = Inf,   use_fast = TRUE,   return_interim = FALSE )

title: Find clusters — find_clusters
text: cases frame case counts location date distance_matrix square distance matrix, named dimensions list distance vectors, one location detect_date date indicates end test window looking clusters spline_lookup default NULL; either spline lookup table, data frame least two columns: including "observed" "spl_thresh", string indicating use one built lookup tables: .e. one "001", "005", "01", "05". NULL, default table 01 (.e. spline_01 dataset) baseline_length integer (default = 90) number days baseline interval max_test_window_days integer (default = 7) number days test window guard_band integer (default = 0) buffer days baseline test interval distance_limit numeric (default=15) maximum distance consider cluster size. Note units value default (miles) unit values distance matrix baseline_adjustment one four string options: "add_one" (default), "add_one_global", "add_test", "none".  methods except "none" ensure log(obs/expected) always defined (.e. avoids expected =0). default, add 1 expected individual calculation expected otherwise zero. "add_one_global", add one baseline location case counts. "add_test_interval", location baseline increased number cases location test interval. "none", adjustment made. adj_constant numeric (default=1.0); constant added baseline_adjustment == 'add_one' baseline_adjustment == 'add_one' min_clust_cases (default = 0); minimum number case within returned cluster. max_clust_cases (default = Inf); maximum number cases within returned cluster. use_fast boolean (default = TRUE) - set TRUE use fast version compress clusters function return_interim boolean (default = FALSE) - set TRUE return interim objects find_clusters() function

title: Find clusters — find_clusters
text: returns list two two dataframes.

title: Find clusters — find_clusters

title: Return baseline and test period case grids restricting by distance — gen_nearby_case_info
Return baseline and test period case grids restricting by distance — gen_nearby_case_info
text: cg object class `CaseGrids`, returned generate_case_grids() distance_matrix square distance matrix, named dimensions list distance vectors, one location distance_limit numeric value indicating distance threshold define "near" locations; must input units distances `distance_matrix`. Note passing list version distance_matrix, limit already used construction thus ignored

title: Return baseline and test period case grids restricting by distance — gen_nearby_case_info
text: object class `NearbyClusterGrids` list two   dataframes, including "baseline" (nearby information baseline   counts) "test" (holds nearby information test interval   counts)

title: Return baseline and test period case grids restricting by distance — gen_nearby_case_info
code: case_grid <- generate_case_grids(   example_count_data, example_count_data[, max(date)] ) nci <- gen_nearby_case_info(   cg = case_grid,   distance_matrix = county_distance_matrix("OH")[["distance_matrix"]],   distance_limit = 25 )

what: Get candidate clusters and locations in baseline intervals — generate_case_grids

text: Given raw case counts location, dates params return candidate clusters counts

title: Get candidate clusters and locations in baseline intervals — generate_case_grids
code: generate_case_grids(   cases,   detect_date,   baseline_length = 90,   max_test_window_days = 7,   guard_band = 0,   baseline_adjustment = c("add_one", "add_one_global", "add_test", "none"),   adj_constant = 1 )

title: Get candidate clusters and locations in baseline intervals — generate_case_grids
text: cases frame cases counts, location(s) dates detect_date date end examination detection clusters baseline_length number days (integer) used baseline detection (default = 90) max_test_window_days integer, max number days detected cluster, defaults 7 guard_band integer (default=0) number days buffer test interval baseline baseline_adjustment one three string options: "add_one" (default), "add_test", "none".  methods except "none" ensure log(obs/expected) always defined (.e. avoids expected =0). default, add 1 expected individual calculation expected otherwise zero. "add_test_interval", location baseline increased number cases location test interval. "none", adjustment made. adj_constant numeric (default=1.0); constant added baseline_adjustment == 'add_one' baseline_adjustment == 'add_one'

title: Get candidate clusters and locations in baseline intervals — generate_case_grids
text: object class `CaseGrids` contain list items `baseline_counts_by_location`: frame counts baseline  interval location `case_grid`: frame cases test dates, reverse  cumulative counts within location, date `case_grid_totals_by_date`: reverse cumulative sum counts   locations, date `test_cases`: case location counts test dates `detect_date`: detect date passed function `baseline_total`: integer holding total counts  locations dates

what: gsClusterDetect: Utilities for Geo-Spatial Cluster Detection and Significance Classification — gsClusterDetect-package
title: gsClusterDetect: Utilities for Geo-Spatial Cluster Detection and Significance Classification — gsClusterDetect-package
text: Provides utilities manipulating time series location-based counts events detect geo-spatial clusters. Significance clusters determined using set models classify based learned relationship observed log(observed/expected) ratio counts. approach implemented similar prospective space-time estimation clusters using scan statistic.

title: gsClusterDetect: Utilities for Geo-Spatial Cluster Detection and Significance Classification — gsClusterDetect-package
text: Maintainer: Luke Mullany luke.mullany@jhuapl.edu Authors: Howard Burkom

title: gsClusterDetect 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-23

title: gsClusterDetect 1.0.0
text: Initial CRAN submission. Provides workflow detecting classifying geospatial clusters count time series location date. Includes end--end cluster detection helpers covering case-grid generation, nearby-case aggregation, observed-versus-expected calculations, identification priority clusters using spline-based threshold classification model learned previous scan statistic runs. Provides distance utilities county, ZIP code, census tract, U.S.-wide, custom point-based locations, including sparse neighbor-list constructors. Includes summary visualization helpers baseline test intervals, including summary tables, heat map data plots, time-series data plots. Includes built-example data spline lookup tables package examples testing.
